title: The faithless who refuse guidance
display: Here 'those who disbelieve' (alladhīna kafarū) does not mean every non-believer, but those set firmly upon denial — whose hearts are overtaken by hostility to the truth and who spare no effort fighting against God's religion. For such people, warning and admonition no longer make a difference.
narration: Here, 'those who disbelieve' — ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا — does not mean every non-believer, but those set firmly upon denial, whose hearts are overtaken by hostility to the truth and who spare no effort fighting against God's religion. For such people, warning and admonition no longer make a difference.

title: The heart — the essence of the human being
display: When the Qur'an speaks of the 'heart' (qalb), it means the very essence of the human being — their life and soul. Just as the body knows health, sickness, and death, so too does the heart: it can be sound, diseased, or dead. Hypocrisy is a sickness of the heart that, left untreated, only spreads.
narration: When the Qur'an speaks of the heart — ٱلْقَلْب — it means the very essence of the human being, their life and soul. Just as the body knows health, sickness, and death, so too does the heart: it can be sound, diseased, or dead. Hypocrisy is a sickness of the heart that, left untreated, only spreads.

title: Shayṭān
display: The word 'shayṭān' (shayṭān) means one far removed from goodness — a being of evil. It is not only Iblīs: because he was driven so far from God's mercy, he became the very embodiment of evil, so that 'shayṭān' came to function almost as a proper name for him — though it can describe any rebellious, corrupting force.
narration: The word شَيْطَان means one far removed from goodness — a being of evil. It is not only إِبْلِيس: because he was driven so far from God's mercy, he became the very embodiment of evil, so that the word شَيْطَان came to function almost as a proper name for him — though it can describe any rebellious, corrupting force.
